author: @benniedonald
text: Great tip and explanation easy to understand. Keep up the good work. 👍

author: @barbufodor1186
text: Good one Luke. An even easier way would be to get a piece of string tied on one end to your tripod/camera/camera tripod plate and on the other end you can make a loop and step on it or put it around your shoe(s). Of course you will need to keep that string under tension. The beauty is that it is more effective (a bit) and very easy to carry it with you anywhere.

Andrea, Jasper and

author: @danielade9694
text: Lowering the center of gravity down the pole will easily make the camera more stable. That is a good cheap way to do it. Buying a commercially made camera stabilizer is quite pricey. Good job at showing cheap DIY solutions that work good. 👍

author: @sorgenteinstabile
text: a little advice here: 4 Kg may seem something light but recording a long shot with that amount of weight may be a little too stressful for the body, causing your movement to "lag", anyway a solution to this problem would be using a weight of about 300-500 grams that is rotating, creating basically a gyroscope under your camera, for personal experience i can tell you that it works greatly and it doesn't stress you as like as 4 kilos

replies.author: @sorgenteinstabile
replies.text: oddjobbob well yeah, it may seems trange but I have worked with a DIY  gyro stabilizer and God it was absolutely amazing being able to record a steady video while basically running

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@sorgenteinstabile Can you please either put a video up, or can you make one for me please?!
Of course I'd be willing to pay. AX

replies.author: @sorgenteinstabile
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt sadly since i moved recently out of my country i don't really know where to buy the materials, otherwise I would have been more than happy to help you with the project

author: @Earzone63
text:
interesting option. my film maker friend used to swear by the 'monopole' method which similar to this. 
I've also seen post processing options that kinda cut corners of each frame out so the net effect is no shaky cam, but you have to sacrifice the edges / pixel resolution

author: @kreynolds1123
text: Turn the weight into a rotating flywheel for even better stabilization.

author: @technosaurus3805
text:
I thought you were going to throw together a gimbal using a few scrap motors, washers, a battery and some scrap metal.
Slightly disappointed, but not enough to downvote.

author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
text: Well done :)  A thing you might also try is additional small weights on each of the tripod leg ends- not a lot,  but it increases stability even more when they're spread out.  Trick an indie filmmaker I know used along with the weighted stand pipe.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: What an awesome suggestion! That would definitely help, thank you mate! :)
